title: Dryopteris otarioides
intro: Dryopteris otarioides, also known as the autumn fern or New Zealand autumn fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential applications in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Dryopteris otarioides include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its purported therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Dryopteris otarioides is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Dryopteris is commonly known as the wood ferns or autumn ferns. Dryopteris otarioides is native to the southeastern United States and is often found growing in shaded, woodland areas. This species of fern is characterized by its lance-shaped fronds and can grow up to several feet in height. In terms of classification, Dryopteris otarioides is a part of the kingdom Plantae and the division Pteridophyta.

In some cultures, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis.
